title: Turret Driver Subsystem Static Attributes
description: Static attributes of the turret driver subsystem, defining hardware parameters for the yaw and/or pitch axes.

max_force.description: Maximum driving torque, in N·m. Also serves as the braking torque.

$schema.description: JSON Schema reference path, used for editor intelligent suggestions and validation

basic_durability.description: Subsystem basic durability. Becomes paralyzed at 0, restores functionality when repaired to 30%.

pass_damage.description: Whether to pass damage to the subsystem holder. If true, damage received by the subsystem is transferred to the part.

limit_damage.description: Whether to limit damage transfer to remaining durability. e.g. 40 damage, 20 remaining, only 20 transfers.

hidden.description: Whether to hide the subsystem in HUD, commonly used for non-functional parts that only provide damage differentiation.

yaw.description: Yaw axis (azimuth) configuration. Enables horizontal turret rotation. Omit to disable yaw control.

pitch.description: Pitch axis (elevation) configuration. Enables vertical turret aiming. Omit to disable pitch control.

control_inputs.description: List of rotation control signal input channels, priority descending. Accepts RotationSignal (x=pitch, y=yaw, z=roll).

on_destroyed.description: Sound event when the subsystem is destroyed.

on_activate.description: Sound event when the subsystem is activated.

examples.description: Yaw-only example - suitable for turret base
Pitch-only example - suitable for barrel elevation
Full example - dual-axis with multiple control signal sources
